history_rule: Closed G, R1, R2, and fkst.task-state/v1 records remain hash-pinned immutable history. Already-dispatched open R2 work completes by append-only v1 transitions whose cutover prefix and each closure re-baseline are verified. New work begins under v2 only after the R3 cutover receipt is activated.

not_authorized: perform a second full package acceptance review

implementation_owner.rule: One fresh owner per package; the same owner performs every correction for that package.

independent_reviewer.rule: One fresh read-only Opus session reviews the exact implementation commit. A materially changed artifact requires another fresh Opus session.

technical_advisor.rule: Fable advises only on architecture, dependency/authority boundaries, plan mutations, live-effect boundaries, milestone reconciliation, and release. Fable does not implement or approve ordinary packages.

human.rule: The human alone grants T1 through T5 using a candidate-bound gate receipt. Silence, timeout, or ambiguous wording is not approval.

mechanical_integration_checks: Sol provider/model/session and all provenance trailers agree
fresh Opus reviewed that exact commit
reviewer has no repository authorship and clean pre/post porcelain
every covered requirement acceptance criterion maps to a command and result
focused and package tests pass at the reviewed commit
changed paths are within the package's declared scope
changed paths do not collide with another active or candidate-integrated package
dependency packages exist on the candidate base
exactly two primary FKST dependencies remain
reference-only packages are absent from imports, runtime graph, and copied production code
Workflow and Council remain app-owned authority
current human tier authorizes the package and every exercised effect
staged integration succeeds in a disposable worktree
candidate smoke, import, graph, and diff checks pass
source, staged, and candidate worktrees are clean after checks

adds: only the exact repository/account/issue/effect envelope, candidate commit, run count, and expiry named by the human receipt
forbids: merge unless separately admitted and explicitly authorized

adds: only the exact approved artifact, tag/release, Hackathon submission, and associated final external actions

fail_closed_rules: A package may not exercise behavior above the current authorization tier.
A pending gate leaves the previous tier as the hard ceiling.
Human approval cannot waive red tests, dependency violations, reference-only imports, reviewer authorship, missing evidence, or unsafe effects.
A regression in a tier's safety mechanism suspends that tier and all higher tiers until same-owner correction, fresh Opus review, and mechanical reintegration.
Every live authorization is target-bound, effect-bound, count-bound, and time-bound; ambiguity consumes the attempt and holds.
